item.title: Ratu Tevita calls for stronger oversight of millions in Lau development funding
item.description: A call has been addressed to the Lau Provincial Council for stronger governance and accountability over millions of dollars in funding available for Lau , with concerns that too much discussion has taken place over the years without enough action. Speaking during the Lau Provincial Council meeting, Turaga na Tui Nayau, Sau ni Vanua ko Lau, Tui Lau Ratu Tevita Uluilakeba Mara says the province has
item.content:encoded:
A call has been addressed to the Lau Provincial Council for stronger governance and accountability over millions of dollars in funding available for Lau , with concerns that too much discussion has taken place over the years without enough action.

Speaking during the Lau Provincial Council meeting, Turaga na Tui Nayau, Sau ni Vanua ko Lau, Tui Lau Ratu Tevita Uluilakeba Mara says the province has the resources to drive its own development, but those resources must be managed properly and used for the benefit of the people.

Ratu Tevita says Lau has a proven history of successful business ventures, recalling that the Provincial Council acquired its first boat through proceeds from the coconut industry, which also played a major role in boosting the province's finances and helping repay outstanding debts.

He says the coconut industry still holds enormous potential because of the strong demand for coconut products in Fiji and could significantly grow Lau's economy.

Ratu Tevita questioned the continued need for provincial levy contributions, saying there are millions of dollars available to the province.

He says the same issues surrounding funding are raised at almost every Provincial Council meeting, but little progress has been made.

Ratu Tevita welcomed the Fiji Development Bank's willingness to safeguard the province's funds and support business growth and development, saying the responsibility now rests with the people of Lau to make the most of the opportunities available.

He also highlighted the funding available through Conservation International Fiji, referring to about $24 million and $11 million , saying the province has access to significant financial resources that should be invested wisely.

Ratu Tevita says the amount of funding available is substantial and stressed it should be used to create lasting development for the province rather than remain idle.

He also raised concerns that the Lau Provincial Council continues to carry debts despite the resources available.

Ratu Tevita called for a proper governance structure and stronger oversight to ensure the available funding is well managed, transparent and delivers real benefits to the people of Lau.

He also acknowledged the Fiji Government's continued support, thanking it for contributing about $33 million in funding and assistance towards the development of Lau Province.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: New market brings relief for Wainunu farmers
item.description: That is now expected to change with the opening of the new Wainunu Farmers Market by Minister for Agriculture, Waterways and Sugar Industry Tomasi Tunabuna. The new market will allow farmers to sell their produce closer to home, reducing transport costs and creating more opportunities to increase their income.
item.content:encoded:
That is now expected to change with the opening of the new Wainunu Farmers Market by Minister for Agriculture, Waterways and Sugar Industry Tomasi Tunabuna.

The new market will allow farmers to sell their produce closer to home, reducing transport costs and creating more opportunities to increase their income.

Around 1,600 people live in the Wainunu district, where farmers have traditionally travelled long distances to reach major markets.

He says the market shows what can be achieved when communities and the Government work together to create opportunities for current and future generations.

The community contributed more than $40,000 towards the construction of the market.

The Ministry of Agriculture provided more than $93,000 through the Commercial Agricultural Development Programme for construction materials and a solar power system.

Tunabuna says the market will provide farmers with a safe and convenient place to sell their produce while creating opportunities for women, young people, entrepreneurs and local vendors.

He says the facility is expected to encourage greater agricultural production and strengthen the local economy.

Organising committee member Akuila Nacoke thanked the Government for supporting the project, saying the new market will make it easier for farmers to sell their produce, reduce transport costs and improve household incomes.

The Minister also reminded the community that the market's long-term success will depend on good management and community ownership.

During a talanoa session after the opening, farmers shared their future plans and raised concerns about agricultural production, market access and infrastructure.

Tunabuna assured them that the Government will continue working with farmers by providing technical advice, production support and agribusiness programmes to improve livelihoods and strengthen food security.

The Wainunu Farmers Market is expected to become a key trading hub for the district while supporting economic growth in the rural community.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Six maritime communities receive boats and engines to improve access to essential services
item.description: For 65-year-old farmer and fisherman Veramu Saumaki of Kavala Village in Kadavu, the new boat and engine will mean safer travel for his family, easier access to Government services, reliable transport for farm produce, and a safer way to take his grandchildren to and from school. The projects were handed over by Minister for Rural and Maritime Development and Disaster Management Mosese Bulitavu un
item.content:encoded:
For 65-year-old farmer and fisherman Veramu Saumaki of Kavala Village in Kadavu, the new boat and engine will mean safer travel for his family, easier access to Government services, reliable transport for farm produce, and a safer way to take his grandchildren to and from school.

The projects were handed over by Minister for Rural and Maritime Development and Disaster Management Mosese Bulitavu under the Ministry's Seafaring Entrepreneurial Assistance Programme.

The Government says it invested about $90,000 in the six projects.

Bulitavu says reliable sea transport is essential for maritime communities, providing access to schools, health centres, markets and workplaces while helping families stay connected.

He says programmes such as the Seafaring Entrepreneurial Assistance Programme are helping rural and maritime communities become more self-reliant and improve their livelihoods.

The Minister also encouraged recipients to take good care of the boats and engines so they can continue to benefit their communities for many years.

Saumaki says the assistance will make travelling safer and easier for his family while improving access to essential services and helping him support his livelihood.

Another recipient, 65-year-old Eremasi Seru of Sawaieke in Gau, thanked the Government for recognising the needs of maritime communities.

He says safe and reliable sea transport is vital for island communities, and the assistance will help improve their daily lives and support their livelihoods.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Man driving alleged stolen vehicle arrested after police interception in Wailoku
item.description: A man allegedly driving a stolen vehicle has been arrested after police intercepted him in Wailoku, damaging a police vehicle during the operation yesterday. Police say the vehicle had been reported stolen from Davuilevu Housing on Thursday, and its details were circulated to officers around the country.
item.content:encoded:
A man allegedly driving a stolen vehicle has been arrested after police intercepted him in Wailoku, damaging a police vehicle during the operation yesterday.

Police say the vehicle had been reported stolen from Davuilevu Housing on Thursday, and its details were circulated to officers around the country.

The vehicle was later spotted in Lami before being seen again in Wailoku, where officers from the Eastern Division Crime Intelligence Unit, Southern Division Operations and the Quick Response Team responded.

According to Police, the driver, who has a known criminal history, tried to flee the scene stressing the officers used a police vehicle to block the suspect's path, resulting in damage to the police vehicle.

The suspect remains in police custody as investigations into the alleged theft continue.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Telecom Fiji upgrading transmission towers to improve connectivity in Taveuni
item.description: Telecom Fiji says the upgrade will strengthen the main connection between Taveuni and the main land of Vanua Levu, while increasing bandwidth capacity to improve internet and mobile services for customers and communities. They say that they will also continue upgrading their existing 4G network to 5G as part of their 5G licence requirements.
item.content:encoded:
Telecom Fiji says the upgrade will strengthen the main connection between Taveuni and the main land of Vanua Levu, while increasing bandwidth capacity to improve internet and mobile services for customers and communities.

They say that they will also continue upgrading their existing 4G network to 5G as part of their 5G licence requirements.

The company says the network improvements will be carried out progressively to expand coverage, including to remote and rural areas.

They say the rollout is also being supported through the Government's Universal Service Obligation programme, which helps extend telecommunications infrastructure to rural communities.

Telecom Fiji says they remain committed to improving access to reliable internet services, and ensuring more people across Fiji stay connected.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Flying Fijians lose to Scotland 33-17
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/07/2026 The FIJI Water Flying Fijians lost to Scotland 33-17 in their third Nations Championship match this morning at Scottish Gas Murrayfield.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 19/07/2026

The FIJI Water Flying Fijians lost to Scotland 33-17 in their third Nations Championship match this morning at Scottish Gas Murrayfield.

Scotland made a strong start, with Jonny Gray scoring the opening try to give the hosts a 7-0 lead. Fiji responded a few minutes later through captain Tevita Ikanivere, narrowing the margin to 7-5.

The Flying Fijians then produced a superb counterattack from inside their own 22. Selestino Ravutaumada raced down the touchline before collecting a chip kick to score Fiji's second try, giving the visitors a 10-7 lead.

Scotland continued to press, but Fiji's defence stood firm, denying the hosts on several occasions.

Fiji were then dealt a blow when flanker Lekima Tagitagivalu was shown a yellow card, leaving the side down to 14 men for 10 minutes after conceding a string of penalties.

Despite being a man down, Fiji launched another brilliant attack.

Scotland was caught napping around the ruck, allowing Elia Canakaivata to score and extend Fiji's lead to 17-7.

Fiji's defence came under relentless pressure, but they held on to their 17-7 advantage at halftime.

The second half belonged to a rejuvenated Scottish side, who dominated possession and territory.

The hosts ran in four unanswered tries, with Jamie Dobie scoring a double, while Pierre Schoeman and Scott Cummings also crossed to seal a 33-17 victory.

1' Fiji losing possession with Elia Canakaivata dropping the ball. FJ 0-0 SCO

6' Tevita Ikanivere caught over-side. Scotland goes for the scrum. FJ 0-0 SCO

8' Fiji defending on their line. Eroni Mawi over-side. Scotland gets a penalty. They go for the line-out. FJ 0-0 SCO

9' Fiji's defence holds off Scotland's maul. They get a scrum and a chance to clear from their tryline2. FJ 0-0 SCO

13' Try to Scotland. Jonny Gray. FJ 0-7 SCO

15' Try to Fiji. Tevita Ikanivere. FJ 5-7 SCO

18' Try to Fiji. Selestino Ravutaumada. FJ 10-7 SCO

23' Scotland gets a penalty inside Fiji's territory. They go for another line-out. FJ 10-7 SCO

25' Lekima Tagitagivalu receives a yellow card for tackling the halfback during a ruck. Scotland go the line-out again. FJ 10-7 SCO

28' Great defence from Fiji, holding off a number of attacks from Scotland. They get the scrum and a chance to clear the ball from their territory. FJ 10-7 SCO

33' Try to Elia Canakaivata. FJ 17-7 SCO

42' Scotland gets a penalty, they go another line-out. FJ 17-7 SCO

47' Mistake from Scotland, they lost it forward. Fiji gets a scrum and a chance to run the ball. FJ 17-7 SCO

52' Fiji penalised for no clear release during a tackle. FJ 17-7 SCO

53' Try to Scotland. Pierre Schoeman. FJ 17-14 SCO

58' Scotland gets another scrum inside Fiji's territory. FJ 17-14 SCO

73' Try to Scotland. Scott Cummings. FJ 17-28 SCO

75' Try to Scotland. Jamie Dobbie. FJ 17-33 SCO

item.title: Lau endorses Economic Think Tank as province unveils ambitious development roadmap
item.description: The Lau Provincial Council has endorsed the newly formed Lau Economic Think Tank Group as the economic advisory arm of the Lau Str
